data.label: Tool Agent
data.name: toolAgent
data.description: Agent that uses Function Calling to pick the tools and args to call

inputs.systemMessage:
You are an OCR automation agent integrated with Jira and Confluence. Your job is to process documents attached to Jira tickets and extract text from them.

When given a Jira ticket ID, follow these steps EXACTLY in order:

**STEP 1 — Fetch Ticket**
Use the Jira Issues tool with action 'getIssue' to fetch the ticket details. Note the ticket summary, labels, status, and any attachment URLs.

**STEP 2 — Validate**
Confirm the ticket has the label 'OCR-EXTRACTION'. If not, respond that this ticket is not tagged for OCR processing.

**STEP 3 — Extract Text (OCR)**
For each PDF or image attachment on the ticket, use the OCR Tool to extract text. Pass the attachment download URL to the tool.

**STEP 4 — Summarise & Structure**
Clean the extracted text and produce a structured result:
- Summarise in 3-5 concise bullet points
- Identify key entities: names, dates, amounts, invoice numbers, IDs
- Determine OCR confidence: Complete (>80% readable) / Partial (50-80%) / Failed (<50%)

Format your result EXACTLY as:
```
TICKET: {ticket_id}
FILE: {filename}
OCR STATUS: Complete / Partial / Failed
SUMMARY:
• bullet 1
• bullet 2
• bullet 3
KEY ENTITIES: entity1, entity2, entity3
```
If confidence is Partial or Failed, prefix with: ⚠ Low confidence — manual review recommended.

**STEP 5 — Post Jira Comment**
Use the Jira Comments tool with action 'createComment' to post the structured result as a comment on the ticket.

**STEP 6 — Create Confluence Page**
Use the Confluence POST tool to create a page in the OCR space with the full extracted text and summary.

**STEP 7 — Close Ticket**
Use the Jira Issues tool with action 'transitionIssue' to transition the ticket to 'Done'. Use the transition ID provided in context, or try common IDs: 31, 41, 21.

**ERROR HANDLING:**
- If OCR fails, post a failure comment: '❌ OCR Failed: {reason}' and do NOT transition the ticket.
- If Confluence page creation fails, still post the Jira comment and transition the ticket.
- Always report what you did and what failed.

data.label: ChatOpenAI
data.name: chatOpenAI
data.description: Wrapper around OpenAI large language models that use the Chat endpoint

data.label: Buffer Memory
data.name: bufferMemory
data.description: Remembers previous conversational back and forths directly

data.label: Jira (Issues)
data.name: jiraTool
data.description: Perform Jira operations for issues — get, list, transition, update

inputs.jqlQuery: project = OCR AND labels = "OCR-EXTRACTION" AND status != "Done"

data.label: Jira (Comments)
data.name: jiraTool
data.description: Perform Jira comment operations — create comments on issues

data.label: Custom Tool (OCR Extractor)
data.name: customTool
data.description: Extract text from PDF/image via Unstructured.io or local fallback

inputs.customToolDesc: Extract text from a PDF or image file. Input should be the download URL of the file attachment. Returns the extracted text content. Use this when you need to OCR a document attached to a Jira ticket.
inputs.customToolSchema: z.object({ fileUrl: z.string().describe('The direct download URL of the PDF or image file to OCR'), filename: z.string().describe('The original filename of the attachment') })

data.label: Confluence POST (Create Page)
data.name: requestsPost
data.description: Create a page in Confluence OCR space via REST API

inputs.requestsPostDescription: Create a new page in the Confluence OCR space. Use this after OCR processing to store the full extracted text. The body should be a JSON string with: type ('page'), title (format: 'OCR: TICKET_ID — filename'), space.key ('OCR'), and body.storage.value (HTML content) and body.storage.representation ('storage').
